content:
<!-- type: requirements lang: markdown -->

| ID | Title | Priority | Acceptance Criteria |
|----|-------|----------|---------------------|
| R1 | @classmethod receives `cls` as first parameter | P1 | `class Foo: @classmethod def bar(cls): return cls.__name__` returns `"Foo"` — matches CPython 3.12 |
| R2 | @property getter/setter/deleter dispatch | P1 | `class C: @property def x(self): return self._x` — read access calls getter, write calls setter, delete calls deleter — no codegen crash |
| R3 | getattr/setattr/delattr emit valid Cranelift IR | P1 | `getattr(obj, 'x')`, `setattr(obj, 'x', 1)`, `delattr(obj, 'x')` all execute without IR verifier error |
| R4 | super().method() propagates return value | P1 | `class B(A): def f(self): return super().f()` — return value from parent method is received by caller, not discarded |
| R5 | Multiple inheritance C3 MRO matches CPython | P1 | `class D(B, C)` where B(A), C(A) — `D.__mro__` == `[D, B, C, A, object]` — matches CPython's `type.mro()` output |

### Constraints

- All fixes must be backward-compatible — existing single-inheritance classes must not regress
- No new public API surface — all changes are internal to codegen and runtime
- Each fix must have a conformance test that runs the same code under CPython 3.12 and Mamba and asserts identical output
